default_tension_pair.selection_reason: No prior experiment shared enough axis context with the incumbent to form a meaningful opposition.

knowledge_selection_reason: No prior experiment shared enough axis context with the incumbent to form a meaningful opposition.
mechanism_hypothesis: Only after we learn whether plain attention can stay competitive should we convert the removed novelty tax into extra depth; otherwise the branch risks mixing simplification and capacity changes before the antithesis is even real.
prediction: Disabling alternating value embeddings while keeping the 8-layer `SSSL` optimizer setup unchanged will slightly improve `val_bpb` because the baseline's extra 16.8M value-embedding parameters are likely a short-horizon novelty tax, so a plainer stack should spend the same 300-second budget on cleaner optimization and a touch more throughput.
takeaway: Experiment is still in flight, so no distilled takeaway is available yet.

tensions.label: Aggression vs stability
tensions.why_active: The incumbent uses zero warmup and aggressive Adam rates, which may be perfect for a 5-minute sprint or may be masking a cleaner optimization regime.

tensions.label: Capacity vs throughput
tensions.why_active: The incumbent already leaves substantial VRAM headroom, but its strong result may still come more from reaching 938 steps and 491.8M tokens than from its nominal parameter count.

tensions.label: Locality vs global context
tensions.why_active: The incumbent's `SSSL` pattern is still an untested compromise between cheaper local attention and full 2048-token coverage.

tensions.label: Value embeddings vs simplicity
tensions.why_active: The incumbent spends roughly one third of its parameters on alternating value embeddings, but no completed local run has shown whether that mechanism actually buys better `val_bpb…

transcendent.concrete_change: If this no-VE negation is competitive, keep `USE_VALUE_EMBEDDINGS = False`, set `DEPTH = 10`, and lower `ASPECT_RATIO` to 48 so the added layers arrive without also widening the model.
